title: Remote Access Conduit Integrity — Split Tunnel on OT VPN Portal

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'IEC 62443-3-2 / Remote access conduit integrity' → 'SR 5.1'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
index=vpn sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" earliest=-7d
| eval split=coalesce(split_tunnel,"unknown")
| lookup ot_vpn_profiles.csv portal OUTPUT tunnel_mode_required
| where tunnel_mode_required="full" AND split!="disabled"
| stats dc(public_ip) as distinct_exit, count by user, portal, split
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user | sort - count
description: Detects GlobalProtect configurations that expose split routing while OT portals should force tunnel-all.
value: Detects GlobalProtect configurations that expose split routing while OT portals should force tunnel-all.
grandmaExplanation: We catch GlobalProtect configurations that expose split routing while OT portals should force tunnel-all — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
implementation: (1) Push portal config from Panorama; (2) block local subnet access lists; (3) monthly config export diff; (4) user re-education.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Palo Alto (2757).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `pan:globalprotect` config fields if logged.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Push portal config from Panorama; (2) block local subnet access lists; (3) monthly config export diff; (4) user re-education.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" earliest=-7d
| eval split=coalesce(split_tunnel,"unknown")
| lookup ot_vpn_profiles.csv portal OUTPUT tunnel_mode_required
| where tunnel_mode_required="full" AND split!="disabled"
| stats dc(public_ip) as distinct_exit, count by user, portal, split
```

Understanding this SPL

**Remote Access Conduit Integrity — Split Tunnel on OT VPN Portal (IEC 62443-3-2 / Remote access conduit integrity)** — Detects GlobalProtect configurations that expose split routing while OT portals should force tunnel-all.

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:globalprotect` config fields if logged. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Palo Alto (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vpn; **sourcetype**: pan:globalprotect. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vpn, sourcetype="pan:globalprotect", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **split** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where tunnel_mode_required="full" AND split!="disabled"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user, portal, split** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Remote Access Conduit Integrity — Split Tunnel on OT VPN Portal (IEC 62443-3-2 / Remote access conduit integrity)** — Detects GlobalProtect configurations that expose split routing while OT portals should force tunnel-all.

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:globalprotect` config fields if logged. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Palo Alto (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Single value (misconfigured sessions), Bar.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
